summary: Analyzed a hypnotic techno, raw techno project with 14 tracks, 109 clips, at 132 BPM. Found 7 potential improvements. Generated 7 completion actions (1 critical, 3 high priority).

actions.title: Extend Arrangement Length
actions.description: Current arrangement is only 4 bars. A full techno track typically runs 128-192 bars for DJ compatibility. Extend the arrangement by repeating groove sections and adding a proper outro.
actions.rationale: At 4 bars the track is too short for most DJ contexts (6-8 minutes minimum at 132 BPM).

actions.title: Add Breakdown Section
actions.description: Insert a 16-32 bar breakdown that removes the kick and bass, keeping only atmospheric elements. This creates contrast and anticipation before the drop.
actions.rationale: Breakdowns are essential for energy management in a DJ set context.

actions.title: Add Bass / Sub Element
actions.description: No bass track detected. Add a sub bass (30-80Hz) that locks to the kick pattern or runs a continuous groove. Consider a single-note bass with filter automation for movement.
actions.rationale: Sub bass provides low-end weight and energy on large sound systems.

actions.title: Add Filter Sweep Automation
actions.description: Automate filter cutoff on key tracks through section transitions. Build from fully closed to 60-70% open over 4-8 bars leading into the drop, and close again entering the breakdown.
actions.rationale: Filter sweeps are the primary energy tool in techno and are expected by listeners.

actions.title: Add Hi-Hat Variation
actions.description: Hi-hat tracks show static repetition. Add open hi-hat accents on off-beats, ghost notes between the main pattern, or introduce velocity variation to add groove and movement.
actions.rationale: Static hi-hats make a groove feel robotic. Subtle variation creates human feel.

actions.title: Add Send Level Automation
actions.description: Automate reverb/delay send amounts on key elements. Throw reverb sends up during breakdowns and transition moments to create space and anticipation.
actions.rationale: Automated send throws create depth and atmosphere essential for techno tension.

actions.title: Add Resonance Automation
actions.description: Automate filter resonance on basslines or synth tracks through peak sections. A rising resonance (Q) from 0.5 to 2.0 creates tension and urgency.
actions.rationale: Resonance automation adds harmonic tension absent from purely gain-based dynamics.

weaknesses: Very little automation detected — arrangement feels static and lacks dynamic movement
No bass/sub track detected — low-end continuity may be weak
2 return track(s) detected but may be underused (no automated sends)
Hi-hat track 'HAT 1': same length clips throughout — no variation or open/closed pattern evolution
Hi-hat track 'HAT 2': same length clips throughout — no variation or open/closed pattern evolution
Hi-hat track 'HAT 3': same length clips throughout — no variation or open/closed pattern evolution
11 tracks with many clips but zero automation — consider adding filter sweeps, send throws, or gain automation
